analysis: Tested a larger 3D model, ResNet-10 from MONAI, within the same geometric pre-training curriculum to see if greater capacity improved generalization. The pre-training loss dropped rapidly, indicating successful learning of the synthetic geometric task. However, fine-tuning on the limited BraTS dataset resulted in a lower validation AUC (0.5349) compared to the Simple3DCNN (0.5967). The increased parameter count of ResNet-10 likely causes severe overfitting, highlighting the benefit of using a simpler, constrained architecture combined with spatial priors for this high-dimensional, low-sample task.
